items.q: How do you say 'I wash myself'?
items.why: Students drop or misuse the reflexive pronoun; reflexive verbs need 'mich/dich/sich'.

items.q: Which reflexive pronoun goes with 'ich' for 'sich anziehen'?
items.why: Students keep 'sich' for all persons; with 'ich' it becomes 'mich' (ich ziehe mich an).

items.q: How do you say 'at half past seven'?
items.why: Students translate 'half past seven' literally; German 'halb acht' means halfway to eight, i.e. 7:30.

items.q: 'Sich anziehen' (to get dressed) is which type of verb?
items.why: Correct answer: A reflexive verb. “A preposition” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Breakfast. “Dinner” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
